mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress equity demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 65% of triggered instances or the t-statistic for the return difference falls below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer and residential demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p greater than 0.10 in quarterly return regressions, or predictive accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices during periods of low inflation confirm robust economic capacity utilization, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% annually, or 20-day returns are negative in more than 60% of high-INDPRO months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real GDP growth reflect expanding aggregate demand that directly boosts corporate revenues, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following data releases.
failure_condition: 5-day post-release returns show no statistical difference from zero with p greater than 0.05 across 10 consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index growth exceeding 3.5 percent annually forces central banks to tighten policy, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 70% of high-inflation months or inflation fails to predict subsequent rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates indicate a strengthening labor market that increases consumer spending power and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines by more than 2% Sharpe ratio or the interaction effect reverses direction in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp declines in housing starts during periods of high household debt signal a looming residential investment collapse that drags down related sector earnings and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing start declines do not precede negative 5-day returns when household debt-to-income ratios are below 100%, or predictive accuracy falls below 48%.
